name: Cottage Cheese Biscuits
recipeIngredient: Cottage cheese (1 1/2 to 2 1/4 cups) -- see note
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, salt, sugar, and baking powder.
2. Cut in the butter or margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add the eggs and 1 1/2 cups of cottage cheese. Mix just enough to bind the ingredients together; use more cottage cheese if needed.
4. Turn onto a lightly floured board and knead very gently a few times.
5. Pat into a 1-inch thickness.
6. Cut into biscuit shapes or divide the dough in half, patting each half into a greased 8-inch round cake pan.
7. Bake at 450°F for 12 to 16 minutes, or until browned. Makes 18 to 20 biscuits (2 inches in diameter).
Note:
You can use any type of creamed cottage cheese; the amount will depend on the moisture content of the cheese. It may vary from about 1 1/2 cups to 2 1/4 cups or even slightly more to create a dough that holds together.
